channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ratings decline follows CBS late-night programming change after Colbert departure
item.description: CBS’s decision to end Stephen Colbert’s tenure on The Late Show and replace the program with a new late-night arrangement under media entrepreneur Byron Allen has been followed by a significant drop i...

item.title: Former Staffer Questions Barry Moore’s Longstanding Claims About Military Service
item.description: U.S. Representative Barry Moore of Alabama, who is running for the U.S. Senate with the endorsement of President Donald Trump, is facing renewed scrutiny over statements regarding his military backgro...

item.title: Trita Parsi Claims Deportation Allegations Were Based on an Unfounded Report and Prompted Public Support
item.description: A controversy emerged after The Free Press published a report alleging that the Trump administration was considering deporting Trita Parsi, a prominent foreign policy analyst, co-founder of the Quincy...

item.title: Rat Eradication on Palau Island Leads to Rapid Recovery of Seabirds, Fish, and Coral Reefs
item.description: A conservation experiment conducted on Ulong Island in Palau has revealed that ecosystems can recover much faster than previously believed after the removal of invasive rats. The project, led by the n...

item.title: Legal Experts Raise Concerns Over Judicial Nominee Justin Smith Ahead of Senate Confirmation Vote
item.description: Justin D. Smith, one of President Donald Trump's attorneys in the E. Jean Carroll litigation, is nearing confirmation to a lifetime position on the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Eighth Circuit. The Se...

item.title: Royal Family marks King Charles III's official birthday during Trooping the Colour celebrations
item.description: Thousands of spectators gathered in central London for the annual Trooping the Colour ceremony, celebrating the official birthday of King Charles III. The event, one of the most significant occasions ...

item.title: Mike Johnson Signals Republican Interest in Reforming Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security
item.description: A political controversy emerged after House Speaker Mike Johnson discussed federal entitlement programs during an interview with conservative radio host Moon Griffon. Johnson argued that the largest d...

item.title: Israel Proposes Temporary Entry Restrictions for Travellers from Five East African Countries Amid Ebola Concerns
item.description: Israel's Ministry of Health has recommended temporary travel restrictions targeting non-citizens and non-residents who have recently visited five East African countries affected by an Ebola outbreak. ...

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Photos Highlight Michelle Pfeiffer and Al Pacino During the Production of Frankie & Johnny (1991)
item.description: A feature published by Vintage Everyday presents a collection of 30 behind-the-scenes photographs from the production of the 1991 romantic comedy-drama Frankie & Johnny, starring Michelle Pfeiffer and...

item.title: Government Introduces Tax Incentives to Expand Electric and CNG Vehicle Use in Tanzania
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has announced a series of tax incentives and policy measures in its 2026/27 budget proposals aimed at accelerating the adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) and compressed nat...

item.title: Best Practices for Running Cypress and Playwright End-to-End Tests in Docker-Based CI Pipelines
item.description: This article explains how to build reliable, containerized end-to-end (E2E) testing environments using Docker, Cypress, and Playwright in continuous integration (CI) systems. The author argues that ru...

item.title: Differences Over Immigration and Identity Emerge Within Vivek Ramaswamy's Ohio Gubernatorial Campaign
item.description: A Politico report highlighted internal tensions within Ohio Republican gubernatorial nominee Vivek Ramaswamy's campaign, revealing disagreements between the candidate's message and the views of some o...

item.title: Economist Richard Wolff Argues U.S.-Iran War Could Accelerate American Global Decline
item.description: The article reports on comments made by economist Richard Wolff, a professor at the University of Massachusetts Amherst, regarding the geopolitical and economic consequences of a U.S. war with Iran. I...

item.title: Opinion Column Criticizes Trump Ahead of His 80th Birthday Over Inflation Remarks and Leadership
item.description: In this opinion article, columnist John Casey uses the occasion of President Donald Trump's upcoming 80th birthday to criticize both Trump's public image and his policies. The author argues that Trump...

item.title: Mary Wickes Played Mary Poppins in a 1949 Television Adaptation Years Before Julie Andrews
item.description: Mary Wickes was the first actress to portray Mary Poppins on screen, appearing in a live television adaptation broadcast by CBS on December 19, 1949. The production aired as part of the anthology seri...

item.title: A Look Back at Taina Elg’s Film and Stage Career During Hollywood’s Golden Age
item.description: This article highlights the life and career of Taina Elg, the Finnish-American actress and dancer who became a notable figure in Hollywood during the 1950s. Before entering the film industry, Elg esta...

item.title: Jesse Ramien scores the opening try for Cronulla Sharks against the Warriors in Auckland
item.description: Jesse Ramien provided the first major moment of the NRL clash between the Cronulla Sharks and the New Zealand Warriors in Auckland, crossing the line to score the opening try of the match. The article...

item.title: Trump Says He Could Seek Greater Federal Control of Washington, D.C., if Socialist Candidate Wins Mayoral Primary
item.description: President Donald Trump stated that he could pursue greater federal control over Washington, D.C., if democratic socialist candidate Janeese Lewis George wins the upcoming Democratic primary for mayor....

item.title: Federal Judge Dismisses University of Florida College Republicans' Free Speech Lawsuit
item.description: A federal judge has dismissed a lawsuit brought by a University of Florida College Republicans group that claimed the university violated its First Amendment rights when it removed the organization fr...

item.title: Sam Ferris Discusses a Decade of Documenting Sydney Through Atmospheric Street Photography
item.description: In this LensCulture interview, Australian photographer Sam Ferris reflects on the development of his long-term street photography project, In Visible Light, which documents Sydney’s central business d...
